title: noExcessiveSelectorClasses
description: Learn more about noExcessiveSelectorClasses
---
import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="CSS" icon="seti:css">
:::note
This rule has been implemented but not released yet. It will be available in the next release.
:::
:::caution
This rule is part of the [nursery](/linter/#nursery) group. This means that it is experimental and the behavior can change at any time.
:::
## Summary
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/nursery/noExcessiveSelectorClasses`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**information**](/reference/diagnostics#information).
- Sources: 
  - Same as [`selector-max-class`](https://github.com/stylelint/stylelint/blob/main/lib/rules/selector-max-class/README.md)

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noExcessiveSelectorClasses": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Limit the number of classes in a selector.

Selectors with too many chained classes are harder to read, harder to override,
and often signal overly specific styling.
This rule enforces an upper bound on how many class selectors can appear in one selector.

Each selector in a selector list is evaluated separately.
For example, `.foo, .bar.baz` is treated as two selectors, and only `.bar.baz`
contributes two class selectors.

Nested selectors are checked as written instead of being resolved against their parent selector.
For example, in `.foo { &.bar {} }`, the nested selector `&.bar` contributes one class selector.

## Examples

### Invalid

The following example will show a diagnostic when `maxClasses` is set to `1`:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noExcessiveSelectorClasses": {
					"options": {
						"maxClasses": 1
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```css
.foo .bar {}
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.css:1:1 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-excessive-selector-classes">lint/nursery/noExcessiveSelectorClasses</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Expected this selector to have no more than 1 class selector, but found 2.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>.foo .bar &#123;&#125;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Selectors with too many chained classes are harder to read, override, and reuse.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Reduce the number of class selectors in this selector, or split it into simpler selectors.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

```css
:is(.foo, .bar.baz) {}
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.css:1:1 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-excessive-selector-classes">lint/nursery/noExcessiveSelectorClasses</a> Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>

### Valid

The following examples are valid when `maxClasses` is set to `1`:

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noExcessiveSelectorClasses": {
					"options": {
						"maxClasses": 1
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```css
.foo {}
```

```css
.foo, div {}
```

## Options

### `maxClasses`

The maximum number of class selectors allowed in a single selector.

This option has no default value. Configure it explicitly to enable the rule.
A value of `0` disallows class selectors entirely.

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noExcessiveSelectorClasses": {
					"options": {
						"maxClasses": 2
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

The following selector exceeds the configured limit because it contains three
class selectors:

```css
.foo .bar.baz {}
```

<pre class="language-text"><code class="language-text">code-block.css:1:1 <a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/rules/no-excessive-selector-classes">lint/nursery/noExcessiveSelectorClasses</a> ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━<br /><br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Expected this selector to have no more than 2 class selectors, but found 3.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: Tomato;">&gt;</span></strong> <strong>1 │ </strong>.foo .bar.baz &#123;&#125;<br />   <strong>   │ </strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><strong><span style="color: Tomato;">^</span></strong><br />    <strong>2 │ </strong><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Selectors with too many chained classes are harder to read, override, and reuse.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">Reduce the number of class selectors in this selector, or split it into simpler selectors.</span><br />  <br />  <strong><span style="color: lightgreen;">ℹ</span></strong> <span style="color: lightgreen;">This rule belongs to the nursery group, which means it is not yet stable and may change in the future. Visit </span><span style="color: lightgreen;"><a href="https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery">https://biomejs.dev/linter/#nursery</a></span><span style="color: lightgreen;"> for more information.</span><br />  <br /></code></pre>
